text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill and welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**DrJill:** {Families|еЃґжЧП|/ЋИf√¶m…™liz/|My family is very close.} can be big.
**DrJill:** Families can be small.

**DrJill:** A family can have a {mother|жѓНи¶™|/ЋИm М√∞…Щr/|My mother is kind.}, {father|зИґи¶™|/ЋИf…СЋР√∞…Щr/|My father works hard.}, {brothers|еЕДеЉЯ|/ЋИbr М√∞…Щrz/|My brothers play soccer.}, and sisters.

**DrJill:** It can have {grandmothers|з•ЦжѓН|/ЋИ…°r√¶nm М√∞…Щrz/|My grandmothers bake cookies.} and {grandfathers|з•ЦзИґ|/ЋИ…°r√¶nf…СЋР√∞…Щrz/|My grandfathers tell stories.}, {aunts|еПФжѓНгГїдЉѓжѓН|/ЋИ√¶nts/|My aunts visit us.}, {uncles|еПФзИґгГїдЉѓзИґ|/ЋИ М≈Лk…Щlz/|My uncles are funny.}, and {cousins|гБДгБ®гБУ|/ЋИk Мz…Щnz/|My cousins play together.}.
**DrJill:** On today's show, our {question|и≥™еХП|/ЋИkwest Г…Щn/|I have a question for you.} is, who is in your family?
**DrJill:** Now let's visit Anna.
**DrJill:** She answers the question.
**DrJill:** Who is in your family?
**Anna:** Hi everyone.
**Anna:** Hi Max
**Max:** Ana, hello!
**Max:** I have our question for today.
**Anna:** Who is in your family?
**Anna:** Who is in my family?
**Anna:** I come from a {big|е§ІгБНгБ™|/b…™…°/|We have a big house.} family.
**Anna:** I have a mother, a father.
**Anna:** three older {brothers|еЕД|/ЋИbr М√∞…Щrz/|My brothers play soccer.}, two older {sisters|еІЙе¶є|/ЋИs…™st…Щrz/|My sisters sing well.}, one younger brother.
**Max:** Your parents have six children?
**Anna:** You are {forgetting|ењШгВМгБ¶гБДгВЛ|/f…ЩrЋИ…°et…™≈Л/|Don't forget your keys.} another child, Max.
**Anna:** Me.
**Anna:** My parents had seven children, but now I live with my {husband|е§Ђ|/ЋИh Мzb…Щnd/|My husband helps me.} and son.
**Max:** And {meepers|гГ°гГЉгГСгГЉгВЇпЉИзМЂгБЃеРНеЙНпЉЙ}, your big black cat.
**DrJill:** How can I forget meepers?
**DrJill:** Anna comes from a big family.
**DrJill:** But now her family is small.
**DrJill:** Who is in your family?
**DrJill:** Who lives with you?
**DrJill:** Sometimes {friends|еПЛдЇЇ|/frendz/|My friends help me.} can be like family.
**DrJill:** And don't forget {pets|гГЪгГГгГИ|/pets/|I have two pets at home.}, they are {family|еЃґжЧП|/ЋИf√¶m…™li/|My family is very close.} too.
**DrJill:** Now let's hear from some {friends|еПЛйБФ|/frendz/|My friends are coming over.}.
**DrJill:** They tell us about their families.
**Students:** My family has four people.
**Students:** My mom, my dad, my {grandpa|з•ЦзИґ|/ЋИ…°r√¶np…СЋР/|My grandpa tells great stories.}, and me.
**Students:** I don't have {brothers|еЕДеЉЯ|/ЋИbr М√∞…Щrz/|I have two brothers.} or sisters.
**Students:** But I have {cousins|гБДгБ®гБУ|/ЋИk Мz…Щnz/|My cousins live far away.}.
**Students:** My family has three people.
**Students:** My mother, my father, and myself.
**Students:** My family has four people.
**Students:** My dad, my mom, my {little|е∞ПгБХгБД|/ЋИl…™tl/|This is a little book.} sister, and me.
**DrJill:** Who do you {look|дЉЉгБ¶гБДгВЛ|/l Кk/|I look like my dad.} like in your family?
**DrJill:** I look like my mother.
**DrJill:** The word {like|гАЬгБЃгВИгБЖгБ™|/la…™k/|He acts like a child.} helps us compare things.
**DrJill:** Another way to compare
**DrJill:** is to add the ending E R.
**DrJill:** For example, you can be {older|еєідЄКгБЃ|/ЋИo Кld…Щr/|My brother is older than me.} or younger than someone.
**DrJill:** You can be {taller|иГМгБМйЂШгБД|/ЋИt…ФЋРl…Щr/|He is taller than his sister.} or shorter.
**DrJill:** Let's go back to Anna.
**DrJill:** She uses both ways of comparing.
**Anna:** This is my son, Felix.
**Anna:** He is {tall|иГМгБМйЂШгБД|/t…ФЋРl/|She is tall and strong.} like me.
**Students:** I'm taller than you and Dad.
**Students:** Now I'm the tallest in the family.
**Anna:** In this picture, you are six years old
**Students:** Now I'm older.
**Max:** How old are you, Felix?
**Students:** I am 17 years old.
**Max:** Wow, you {grew|жИРйХЈгБЧгБЯ|/…°ruЋР/|Plants grow in sunlight.} a lot.
**Anna:** Kids {grow|жИРйХЈгБЩгВЛ|/…°ro К/|Children grow quickly.} so fast.
**Anna:** And he is still {growing|жИРйХЈгБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛ|/ЋИ…°ro К…™≈Л/|He is growing taller every year.}
**Students:** Mom, stop it.

**DrJill:** A {family tree|еЃґз≥їеЫ≥|/ЋИf√¶mli triЋР/|My family tree shows our history.} shows {connections|гБ§гБ™гБМгВК/йЦҐдњВ|/k…ЩЋИnek Г…Щnz/|We have strong family connections.} between family members.
**DrJill:** Here is an example.
**DrJill:** Your {grandmother|з•ЦжѓН|/ЋИ…°r√¶ndm М√∞…Щr/|My grandmother tells great stories.} and {grandfather|з•ЦзИґ|/ЋИ…°r√¶ndf…СЋР√∞…Щr/|My grandfather is very wise.} are on top.
**DrJill:** Under them is their {children|е≠РдЊЫгБЯгБ°|/ЋИt Г…™ldr…Щn/|The children are playing outside.}, your mother or father.
**DrJill:** Then you put your mother and father's {children|е≠РдЊЫгБЯгБ°} under them.

**DrJill:** You can add {uncles|гБКгБШ/гБКгБШгБХгВУ|/ЋИ М≈Лk…Щlz/|My uncles live in different cities.}, {aunts|гБКгБ∞/гБКгБ∞гБХгВУ|/ЋИ√¶nts/|My aunts love to cook.}, and {cousins|гБДгБ®гБУ|/ЋИk Мz…Щnz/|My cousins are fun to play with.}
**DrJill:** You can even put your {pet|гГЪгГГгГИ|/pet/|My pet is very friendly.} on your family tree.
**DrJill:** Now let's go back to Anna's house.
**DrJill:** She makes a family tree for her family.
**Students:** Mom, what are you wearing?
**Anna:** {Felix|гГХгВІгГ™гГГгВѓгВє}, my son, this is our family tree.
**Anna:** Can you put the picture of {grandma|з•ЦжѓН} on my right {branch|жЮЭ/з≥їзµ±|/br√¶nt Г/|This branch of the family is large.}?
**Anna:** Felix, look!
**Anna:** A {bird|й≥•|/b…ЬЋРrd/|A bird flew into the window.} landed on our family tree.
**DrJill:** Now you can talk about your family and you can make your own family tree.
**DrJill:** See you next time on Let's Learn English with Anna
